title: Two Sum Optimized (Hash Map)
problem: Two Sum in O(n) using a hash map. Return [i, j].
concept: Hash map trades O(n) space for O(1) lookup. For each number, check if its complement was already seen. This is THE optimization pattern in algorithm design — precompute + lookup vs brute force search.

title: Max Subarray Sum K (Sliding Window)
problem: Maximum sum of any contiguous subarray of size k.
concept: Fixed sliding window: compute first window, then slide by subtracting the leaving element and adding the entering element. Like a moving average filter on audio — you don't recompute the entire sum each time.
audio_connection: Moving average on audio buffers uses the same sliding window approach

title: Binary Tree Node + Build
problem: Create TreeNode class and build tree: root=1, left=2, right=3, left.left=4, left.right=5.
concept: Binary tree = nodes pointing to other nodes. Foundation for BSTs, heaps, tries, and expression trees. Each node has val, left child, right child. This is the data structure underlying many efficient algorithms.
complexity: O(n) to build where n = number of nodes

title: Max Depth of Binary Tree
problem: Maximum depth of a binary tree. Empty=0, single node=1.
concept: Tree recursion: decompose into subtrees. Base case: null → 0. Recursive case: 1 + max of children. This two-line solution encapsulates the power of recursion — the call stack tracks all the work.
complexity: O(n) time, O(h) space where h = height (recursion stack)

title: In-Order Traversal
problem: Return values from in-order traversal (left, root, right).
concept: In-order visits BST nodes in sorted order: left subtree, current node, right subtree. Pre-order (root,left,right) for copying. Post-order (left,right,root) for deletion. These three traversals plus BFS cover every tree problem.

title: Valid Palindrome
problem: Is string a palindrome? Ignore non-alphanumeric, case insensitive.
concept: Two pointers converging from edges: compare left and right, move inward. O(n) time, O(1) space (after cleaning). This is the same converging pattern used in array reversal, container with most water, and partitioning.
complexity: O(n) time, O(1) space after preprocessing

title: Frequency Counter
problem: Return object: keys=elements, values=count of appearances.
concept: Frequency map is the building block of hash map problems. Pattern: if key exists increment, else initialize to 1. The (f[x]||0)+1 idiom is essential JS. Used in anagrams, top-K, and counting problems.
complexity: O(n) time, O(k) space where k = unique elements

title: Valid Parentheses
problem: Is the bracket string valid? (){}[] must match correctly.
concept: Stack = LIFO. Perfect for nested matching: push opens, pop and compare on close. If stack empty at end AND every close matched, valid. This pattern extends to expression evaluation, HTML parsing, and syntax validation.

title: Merge Sorted Arrays
problem: Merge two sorted arrays into one sorted array. No .sort().
concept: Two pointers on two sorted sequences: always pick the smaller. This is the merge step of merge sort. O(n+m) time. Handle remaining elements with two while loops.

title: Binary Search
problem: Find index of target in sorted array. Return -1 if not found.
concept: O(log n): eliminate half the search space each step. Works on any monotonic function, not just sorted arrays. The l<=r condition and l=m+1/r=m-1 boundaries are error-prone — get them right and binary search works everywhere.

title: Build Linked List
problem: Create ListNode class and build linked list from array. Return head.
concept: Simplest pointer-based structure. Each node has val and next pointer. Build by creating head then linking subsequent nodes. Foundation for stacks, queues, and graph adjacency lists.

title: Reverse Linked List
problem: Reverse singly linked list in place. Return new head.
concept: Three-pointer technique: prev, current, next. Redirect each node's .next to point backward. Most common linked list interview question. Must save next before overwriting .next.

title: Fibonacci with Memoization
problem: Nth Fibonacci with memoization. fib(0)=0, fib(1)=1.
concept: Memoization: cache results of expensive function calls. Transforms O(2^n) exponential recursion into O(n) linear by never recomputing the same subproblem. This IS dynamic programming — top-down approach.

title: Longest Substring Without Repeating
problem: Length of longest substring without repeating characters.
concept: Variable sliding window + hash map. Expand right pointer always. When duplicate found, jump left pointer past previous occurrence. Track max window size. Top interview question — understand this pattern deeply.
complexity: O(n) time, O(k) space where k = charset size

title: Group Anagrams
problem: Group strings that are anagrams together.
concept: Canonical form as hash key: sort each string to normalize anagrams to same key. Group by key. This 'normalize then group' pattern works for any equivalence relation.
complexity: O(n * k log k) time where k = max string length

title: Product Except Self
problem: Each element = product of all other elements. No division. O(n).
concept: Prefix + suffix products: two passes. Left pass builds prefix products, right pass multiplies by suffix products. Each element gets product of everything to its left times everything to its right — without itself.
complexity: O(n) time, O(1) extra space (output array not counted)

title: Detect Cycle in Linked List
problem: Return true if linked list has a cycle.
concept: Floyd's tortoise and hare: slow pointer moves 1, fast moves 2. If cycle exists, they must meet. If no cycle, fast reaches end. O(n) time, O(1) space — no extra data structure needed.

title: Merge Sort
problem: Implement merge sort.
concept: Divide and conquer: split in half, recursively sort each half, merge. O(n log n) guaranteed — stable sort. The merge step is the same two-pointer merge from challenge #27. Must-know for interviews.
complexity: O(n log n) time, O(n) space

title: Path Sum
problem: Root-to-leaf path exists that sums to target?
concept: Reduce the target as you recurse: subtract current node's value. At leaf, check if remaining equals node value. This 'shrinking parameter' approach works for many recursive tree problems.

title: Kadane's Algorithm (Max Subarray)
problem: Maximum sum of any contiguous subarray. Array can have negatives.
concept: At each position: extend current subarray or start fresh. If current sum becomes negative, starting fresh is always better. This is DP as a simple loop — cur is the state, the max() decision is the transition.

title: First Unique Character
problem: Index of first non-repeating character. -1 if none.
concept: Two-pass frequency pattern: first pass counts, second pass finds first with count 1. Two passes is fine — still O(n). This pattern separates data collection from decision making.

title: Queue From Two Stacks
problem: Implement FIFO queue using two stacks.
concept: Reversing a stack gives FIFO order. Lazy transfer: only move s1→s2 when s2 is empty. Amortized O(1) per operation because each element is moved at most twice.
